channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nearly 300 deportees from US held in Panama hotel as officials try to return them to their countries
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama is detaining in a hotel nearly 300 people from various countries deported under U.S. President Donald Trump, not allowing them to leave while waiting for international authorities to organize a return to their countries.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A$AP Rocky dives into Rihanna's arms as not guilty verdict is read at felony assault trial
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A$AP Rocky dove into the arms of Rihanna Tuesday as a clerk read the not guilty verdict at his trial on two felony counts of assault with a semiautomatic firearm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's firing of 1,000 national park workers raises concerns about maintenance and operating hours
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees who maintain and clean parks, educate visitors and perform other functions as part of its broad-based effort to downsize government.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can't afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: JFK Presidential Library closes amid Trump federal worker culling
item.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum closed on Tuesday and will remain closed until further notice, a notice from its foundation read.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP pushes ahead with budget bill that funds Trump's mass deportations and border wall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans pushed ahead late Tuesday on a scaled-back budget bill, a $340 billion package to give the Trump administration money for mass deportations and other priorities, as Democrats prepare a counter-campaign against the onslaught of actions coming from the White House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration gives schools a deadline to end DEI programs or risk losing federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is giving America’s schools and universities two weeks to eliminate diversity initiatives or risk losing federal money, raising the stakes in the president’s fight against “ wokeness.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says AP will continue to be curtailed at White House until it changes style to Gulf of America
item.description: President Donald Trump says his administration will continue to restrict The Associated Press' access to cover presidential events until the news agency goes along with his renaming of the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury finds A$AP Rocky not guilty in felony assault trial
item.description: A verdict has been reached in the felony assault trial of rapper A$AP Rocky: NOT GUILTY. Jurors announced they’d made a decision just hours after the deliberations began Tuesday morning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Sheriff says 3 kids found 'basically abandoned' for years at suburban Detroit home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donations of clothes and money are pouring in for three children who were “basically abandoned” by their mother for years, living alone in a suburban Detroit home among trash and feces, a sheriff said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter, who forgave gunman's mother, dies at 43
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Anne Marie Hochhalter, who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting but found strength to forgive and to heal her soul after bonding with another family devastated by the tragedy, has died. She was 43. Hochhalter was found in her home in suburban Denver on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Catholic bishops sue Trump administration for halt in funding for refugee settlement
item.description: Catholic bishops sued the Trump administration on Tuesday over its abrupt halt to funding of refugee resettlement, calling the action unlawful and harmful to newly arrived refugees and to the nation's largest private resettlement program. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: 3 victims killed in Green River Tunnel crash identified, 18 people treated for injuries
item.description: The three people who died in a fiery crash in Wyoming's Green River Tunnel on Friday, Feb. 14 have been identified by officials.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Chutkan refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows ICE preparing illegal immigrants to be flown out of Seattle airport
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, or ICE, prepared a group of illegal undocumented immigrants to be flown back to their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Southwest Airlines cutting hundreds of jobs
item.description: Southwest Airlines announced Monday that it will cut 1,750 jobs in late April in what will be the first round of layoffs in the company’s 53-year history.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Postmaster general to step down, asks for replacement
item.description: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy, a former prominent Trump fundraiser who was appointed during the president's first term, announced Tuesday he would be stepping down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to 6 killings in multiple states
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fugitive jailed after melting hospital bed rail to escape
item.description: A man who was wanted for allegedly trying to shoot a gas station employee last year is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Man drives 730 miles to burn down ex-girlfriend's new boyfriend's house, police say
item.description: "At the time the fire started, all the residents in the house were sleeping. This is obviously a nightmare for anyone," an official said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Southwest cutting nearly 1,800 jobs, airline says
item.description: Southwest Airlines plans to lay off more than 1,700 employees in a cost-cutting move the company’s CEO described as “unprecedented” in the airline’s 53-year history, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Colorado (AP) — Santa Rosa. Paradise. Boulder County. Lahaina. Los Angeles. All are places that have shown that American cities and their water systems weren't built to withstand wildfire, experts say. Hydrants trickled. Pumps and treatment plants lost power.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan mom who braved flames for son in hyperbaric chamber explosion stresses treatment dangers: attorney
item.description: The Michigan mom of 5-year-old Thomas Cooper, who died in a hyperbaric chamber on Jan. 31, jumped toward flames in the chamber to try to save her son after it exploded.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Firefighters battle massive blaze at Pennsylvania business
item.description: A massive fire broke out at a building in Abington Township, just outside of Philadelphia, Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
